items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are you still up for hiking Pine Ridge on Saturday? I checked the forecast and it's supposed to be sunny.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. But I heard the main trailhead lot fills up by eight, so we'd have to leave really early.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, eight? I was hoping to sleep in a little.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, there's a shuttle from the rec center that runs all morning. We could just take that instead of driving.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds great, actually. No parking stress. What time does the first one leave?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Around nine, I think. Let me double-check the rec center website tonight and text you the schedule.

questions.stem: What are the two speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to get to a hiking trail on the weekend
options.B: Whether the weekend weather will be good enough to hike
options.C: Which hiking trail is the most challenging one nearby
options.D: How to sign up for a guided hiking tour

questions.explanation: 对话主线是周六去 Pine Ridge 徒步以及如何到达——是开车赶早占车位还是坐 rec center 的班车，故 A 正确。B 是 too_narrow：天气只在开头一句被顺带提到，不是核心话题。C 是 wrong_topic：从未比较过哪条路线最难。D 是 wrong_topic：没有任何关于报名向导团的内容。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do on Saturday?

options.A: Arrive at the trailhead lot before eight o'clock
options.B: Cancel the trip if the parking lot is full
options.C: Take the rec center shuttle to the trail
options.D: Drive separately and meet at the summit

questions.explanation: 女生对班车方案说 'That sounds great'，男生还要发班车时刻表，可推断他们会坐班车，故 C 正确。A 是 rejected_option：女生不想那么早，两人转而选择班车，所以早于八点到停车场被否决了。B 是 over_inference：没人提到要取消行程。D 是 unrelated_next_step：对话中没有分开开车或在山顶碰面的任何依据。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for the dinner party, I'm thinking we set out a cheese board before the main course. What should we grab?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let's get a soft brie and a sharp cheddar. Oh, and some crackers to go with them.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good call. Should we add grapes too? They always look nice on the board.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, grapes work. But skip the blue cheese this time—remember, Tom can't stand the smell.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right, I forgot about that. Okay, brie, cheddar, crackers, and grapes. I'll stop by the market after my last class.

questions.stem: Which item do the speakers decide NOT to buy?
questions.explanation: 男生明确说 'skip the blue cheese this time'，因为 Tom 受不了它的气味，故 B 是他们决定不买的，正确。A、C、D 都被明确列入购买清单（饼干、切达、葡萄），所以都是 wrong_detail：把要买的说成不买的。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Ask Tom which cheeses he prefers
options.C: Borrow a cheese board from a neighbor
options.D: Go to the market to buy the food

questions.explanation: 女生最后说 'I'll stop by the market after my last class'，可推断她接下来会去市场买东西，故 D 正确。A 是 unrelated_next_step：他们已替 Tom 做了决定，没说要再问他。B 是 over_inference：完全没有取消聚会的迹象。C 是 unrelated_next_step：对话从未提到借奶酪拼盘。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening class one of them took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I just got back from that weekend gardening class at the campus greenhouse. It was so much fun.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh nice! What did you actually do there?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: We each potted our own herb garden—basil, mint, and parsley. They let us take the pots home.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's a great deal. Was it hard to get a spot? I've heard those fill up fast.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, yeah. I signed up the day registration opened. You should grab a spot early if you want in next month.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll definitely do that. I've been wanting to grow something on my windowsill.

questions.stem: What did the woman do in the gardening class?

options.A: She learned to design a flower garden
options.B: She watched a demonstration on composting
options.C: She potted her own herb garden to take home
options.D: She helped plant trees around the greenhouse

questions.explanation: 女生说 'We each potted our own herb garden... They let us take the pots home'，故 C 正确。A 是 wrong_detail：种的是香草盆栽，不是设计花园。B 是 related_but_not_stated：堆肥从未被提及。D 是 related_but_not_stated：种树在对话中没有出现。

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest the man do?

options.A: Register early for next month's class
options.B: Buy his herbs from a local nursery instead
options.C: Ask the instructor for private lessons
options.D: Join her in the greenhouse this weekend

questions.explanation: 女生说名额很快满，建议 'grab a spot early if you want in next month'，故 A 正确。B 是 unrelated_next_step：她从未建议去苗圃买香草。C 是 over_inference：没有提到私人课程。D 是 wrong_detail：课程在下个月，不是本周末和她一起去。

items.situation: a student asking a friend for recommendations on a new couch for the dorm

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You got a couch for your dorm last semester, right? I want one, but mine has to fit through that narrow doorway.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, that's exactly why I didn't go with a big sectional. I got a futon that folds flat, so it slid right in.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, a futon. Is it comfortable enough to actually sit on every day, though?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Mine is, but honestly the cheap ones sag fast. I'd spend a bit more and check the frame is metal, not wood.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay. And where did you even find one that wasn't huge?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The home store on Fifth Street has compact ones. I'd measure your doorway first, then go look this weekend.

questions.stem: Why is the woman talking to the man?

options.A: To complain that her current couch is uncomfortable
options.B: To ask him to help move furniture into her dorm
options.C: To find out where the nearest furniture store is
options.D: To get advice on choosing a couch for her dorm

questions.explanation: 女生说想买沙发，并不断向有经验的男生请教款式、质量和购买地点，故主旨是征求买沙发的建议，D 正确。A 是 reversed_situation：她还没有沙发，谈不上抱怨现有的不舒服。B 是 wrong_topic：从未请他帮忙搬家具。C 是 too_narrow：商店位置只是众多建议中的一条，不是整体目的。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do before buying a couch?

options.A: Order a large sectional sofa online
options.B: Measure the width of her doorway
options.C: Borrow the man's futon for a week
options.D: Ask the dorm office for a couch

questions.explanation: 男生最后建议 'measure your doorway first, then go look'，而女生整段都担心门窄，可推断她会先量门，故 B 正确。A 是 rejected_option：男生正因门窄而放弃大型组合沙发，她也不会去订。C 是 unrelated_next_step：没有任何借用对方沙发的提议。D 是 unrelated_next_step：对话中从未提到向宿舍办公室要沙发。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I dropped my phone and the whole screen is cracked. Can you fix it here today?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me take a look. Ah, it's a deep crack. We can replace the screen, but we're out of this model's part right now.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. How long would it take to get the part in?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We'd order it overnight, so it'd be ready by tomorrow afternoon. The repair itself takes about an hour once it's here.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That works. Do I need to leave the phone with you now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No, hang on to it. I'll text you the moment the part arrives, and you can bring it in then.

questions.stem: Why does the woman go to the shop?

options.A: To have a cracked phone screen repaired
options.B: To buy a brand-new phone of the same model
options.C: To return a phone she recently purchased
options.D: To ask about extending her phone's warranty

questions.explanation: 女生开头就说手机摔了屏幕全裂，问能否修，故 A 正确。B 是 wrong_topic：她要修屏，不是买新机。C 是 reversed_situation：她不是来退货的。D 是 wrong_topic：保修延长从未被提及。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do after this conversation?

options.A: Leave her phone at the shop today
options.B: Buy the replacement part herself online
options.C: Wait for a text before returning to the shop
options.D: Go to a different shop for a faster repair

questions.explanation: 男生说零件到货时会发短信通知她再带手机过来，可推断她会等短信再回店，故 C 正确。A 是 rejected_option：男生明确说 'hang on to it'，不必现在留下手机。B 是 unrelated_next_step：自己网购零件从未被提及。D 是 over_inference：她已说 'That works'，没有迹象要换别家。
